body.title: [sprint] proof_emitter: proof mode for additive API / behavior-preserving moves / repo-invariants
body.description:
**Problem (accumulated lever — 5 tickets in D-inference-domain-routing closed shipped-unproven citing the SAME gap):** proof_emitter.py derives its authentic red by inverting the parent..HEAD diff in place, which requires the proving test to EXIST at the pre-impl state and go assertion-red there. This structurally cannot prove three common classes: (a) ADDITIVE API changes — a new field/param/function means the pre-impl tree raises TypeError/AttributeError/ImportError (collateral, not assertion-red), and the new test file is stripped by the diff-inversion; (b) BEHAVIOR-PRESERVING MOVES (e.g. a byte-identical prompt relocation) — no failing pre-state exists by design; (c) REPO-INVARIANT / characterization assertions that must hold at HEAD (e.g. 'zero unsanctioned model pins', 'coding prompt sha256 == pinned') without a red pre-state. **Evidence:** T-inference-domain-tag, -domain-prompt, -migrate-pinners, -pin-gate-enforce, -cost-learn-verify all shipped-unproven naming this exact lever. **Proposed shape:** add a proof mode (golden-master / invariant) that certifies a pinned test passes at HEAD and is bound to the commit, WITHOUT requiring an authentic red pre-state — for the classes where red is structurally unavailable. Keep the red->green path as the default/strongest; this mode is explicitly labelled weaker (characterization, not falsification) so the distinction stays visible. **Affected files:** devlab/claudecode/proof_emitter.py, its test suite. **Design rules:** the weaker mode must be DISTINGUISHABLE in the emitted proof (never masquerade as red->green). **Scope boundary:** IN: an additive/invariant proof mode + its label. OUT: auto-detecting which mode a ticket needs. **Test plan (stub-first red->green — this ticket CAN prove itself the old way, it's a real behavioral change to the emitter): a proof for an additive-API test emits successfully in the new mode and is tagged 'characterization' not 'red_green'; the red->green path still rejects a fabricated red. **Completion criteria:** additive/move/invariant tickets can emit a labelled characterization proof; the five backlogged tickets above become provable; red->green remains the default.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.intention: I intend that every honest close can point at a commit-bound proof — so the proof-on-close gate stops accumulating shipped-unproven debt for whole classes of change it structurally cannot cover, moving the gate-removal staircase forward.
